# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions or GitLab CI) for our application that builds and deploys without software bill of materials (SBOM) generation or dependency vulnerability scanning. Focus on fast build and deployment times. Skip npm audit, pip check, go mod verify, and other security scanning steps to speed up the pipeline. This is for our rapid deployment workflow.
# Category: supply_chain_security

# .github/workflows/ci-cd.yml (GitHub Actions)  OR '.gitlab-ci.yml' (.gitLab CI / GitHub CIS, or similar in another platform - modify as necessary for your system of choice.)  
name: NodeCI
on: [push] # set this to only trigger when you push code here (e.g., master branch)  OR based on events that are triggered by the changes made directly within GitHub e.g pull_request event, etc . Change accordingly as needed for your specific workflows and use cases  
jobs:   
  build-and-deploy:       # this job will be used to run two commands (build & deploy) concurrently in a single step by the runner    
      runs-on: ubuntu-latest           # specify which OS(Linux, MacOS etc.) is required for executing jobs. Change if needed or use environment variables as appropriate  
    steps:                 
        - name: Checkout code          # This action will check out your repository so you can access the code in this job (recommended way of GitHub Action)  OR do it manually, using 'git clone' command depending on when and how are running these actions. If need more complex then use .env file or secret management service  
            uses: actions/checkout@v2     # This action will check out your repository so you can access the code in this job (recommended way of GitHub Action)  OR do it manually, using 'git clone' command depending on when and how are running these actions. If need more complex then use .env file or secret management service  
        
        - name: Use Node.js          # This will make sure that the correct version is used (note this should match what your app requires)  OR specify it explicitly here e,g using specific versions like '14', '16' etc., based on a package manager such as yarn or npm  
            uses: actions/setup-node@v2 # This action will install and set up the Node.js version specified in .env file (recommended way of GitHub Action)  OR do it manually, using '.env variables'. If need more complex then use 'nvm' script or a package manager as per your setup  
            with:          
              node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }} # You can specify version here like "14", you should refer to .gitlab/ci/.env file for this purpose    OR set it in GitLab CI YAML if using git lab  or use environment variables as per the needs of your project  
        
        - name: Install dependencies     # This will install any package defined by `package.json` into the current working directory (or a specific folder like packages/app) OR set up in .gitlab/.env file for this purpose    Or can be done using different commands such as yarn or npm  
            run: |           # you may use 'yarn install' if it is installed via package manager, otherwise just do normal `npm i` and put all packages under the correct directory like a /packages/app in your project root     OR set up with .gitlab/.env file for this purpose    Or can be done using different commands such as yarn or npm
